extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by Rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is the conjunction of all 10 conditions INCLUDING prior_gi_bleed. rec_drug_b is set true by Rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever prior_gi_bleed is true. The two rules target different outputs, so there is no priority/tie conflict that would suppress either — each fires independently and sets its own output. Crucially, prior_gi_bleed is a conjunct of Rule rec_a_from_conjunction, so ANY input that makes rec_drug_a true necessarily has prior_gi_bleed=true, which in turn makes Rule rec_b_from_trigger fire and set rec_drug_b=true. Therefore rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are jointly true whenever the full conjunction holds. Setting all 10 conjunction inputs to true (diabetes irrelevant, set false for minimality) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property. The defaults do not help because both rules positively set their outputs to true. Hence the property is violated.
